steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: The expression $10x^2 + 4 + 2x^2$ has two $x^2$ terms: $10x^2$ and $2x^2$. The constant 4 has no like term.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $12x^4 + 4$: Adds the coefficients correctly (12) but raises the exponent to 4 — adding terms does not change the exponent. ✗
  • Choice B — $12x^2 + 4$: $10x^2 + 2x^2 = 12x^2$; the constant 4 remains. ✓
  • Choice C — $16x^4$: Adds 4 to the coefficient (giving 16) and changes the exponent to 4 — both errors. ✗
  • Choice D — $16x^2$: Absorbs the constant 4 into the coefficient ($12+4=16$) instead of keeping it separate. ✗
That is the correct answer.
